title: Speaking: The Delhi Auto Ride
learningGoal: Verbally command an auto driver using formal imperatives and negotiate prices aloud.
instructions: You are roleplaying a tourist in an auto-rickshaw. You must give clear directions to the driver and negotiate the final price. Use 'Aap' (formal) commands to remain respectful.
speakingPrompt: Respond to the prompts to guide the auto driver and settle the bill.
scenario: Guiding an auto-rickshaw to a hotel and negotiating the fare.

subTasks.prompt: Tell the driver: 'Brother, please take a right from ahead.' (Brother = Bhaiya, From ahead = Aage se, Right = Daayein, Take = Leejiye)

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Brother, please take a right from ahead.

subTasks.prompt: The driver is about to go left. Stop him! Say: 'Do not go left! Please go straight.' (Left = Baayein, Do not go = Mat jaiye, Straight = Seedha, Go = Chaliye)

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Do not go left! Please go straight.

subTasks.prompt: You arrived. Say: 'Please stop here. How much money did it become?' (Here = Yahaan, Stop = Rokiye, How much money = Kitne paise, Become = Hue)

sampleResponse.baseTranslation: Please stop here. How much money is it?

subTasks.prompt: He asks for 500 rupees. Say: 'This is very expensive. Please apply the right price.' (Expensive = Mehenga, Right price = Sahi daam, Apply = Lagaiye)

sampleResponse.targetText: यह बहुत महँगा है। सही दाम लगाइए।
sampleResponse.transliteration: Yeh bahut mehenga hai. Sahi daam lagaiye.
sampleResponse.baseTranslation: This is very expensive. Please apply the right price.

baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Brother, please take a right from ahead.
Do not go left! Please go straight.
Please stop here. How much money is it?

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: यह बहुत महँगा है। सही दाम लगाइए।
baselineTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Yeh bahut mehenga hai. Sahi daam lagaiye.
baselineTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: This is very expensive. Please apply the right price.

acceptableParaphrases: Saying 'Yahaan rukiye' is acceptable, though 'rokiye' is better for transitive stopping of a vehicle.

criteria.name: Nahi Error
criteria.description: If user says 'Baayein nahi jaiye', deduct massive points. Must use 'mat'.

criteria.name: Casual Command
criteria.description: If user says 'Daayein lo' or 'Seedha chalo' instead of 'leejiye/chaliye', deduct points for not using formal register.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Bhaiya, aage se daayein leejiye. Baayein mat jaiye! Seedha chaliye. Yahaan rokiye. Kitne paise hue? Yeh bahut mehenga hai. Sahi daam lagaiye.

feedbackRules.onPass: Incredible! You handled the auto driver perfectly with firm, polite commands.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review Section 1 and 2. Make sure every command ends in 'iye' (Leejiye, Chaliye, Rokiye).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Watch out for negative commands. Never use 'nahi' to tell someone not to do something. Use 'mat'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: भैया, आगे से दाएँ लीजिए। बाएँ मत जाइए! सीधा चलिए। यहाँ रोकिए। कितने पैसे हुए? यह बहुत महँगा है। सही दाम लगाइए।

hintText: Speak loudly and clearly. Command the space with 'Leejiye' and 'Chaliye'.
